unitPolicies.reason: The JSON-RPC/session API entrypoint depends on internal/api, which is outside the standalone compiler runtime port.
fmtMain is the commented formatter command path and depends on internal/format / language-service formatter settings, which are outside the standalone compiler runtime port.

runtimeAdaptation.reason: The stateful Go reader is stored as one JavaScript class instance while pointer values retain aggregate object identity.
The stateful Go writer is stored as one JavaScript class instance while pointer values retain aggregate object identity.
The public options alias is represented by the structural JavaScript option records consumed by this runtime.

runtimeAdaptation.nominalityReason: The structural operation interface intentionally erases the distinct Go decoder type identity.
runtimeAdaptation.reason: The decoder is exposed through its structural operation contract while pointer values retain aggregate object identity.

runtimeAdaptation.nominalityReason: The structural operation interface intentionally erases the distinct Go encoder type identity.
runtimeAdaptation.reason: The encoder is exposed through its structural operation contract while pointer values retain aggregate object identity.

runtimeAdaptation.nominalityReason: The unbranded string storage intentionally erases the distinct Go token-kind type identity.

The stateful JSON token value is stored as one JavaScript class instance with aggregate object identity.

runtimeAdaptation.nominalityReason: The structural method interface intentionally erases the distinct Go hasher type identity.

The language tag is stored as a branded BCP-47 string and pointer writes replace the containing scalar slot.

Go test files must be ported through the test-parity harness, not production source scaffolding.
Node test mirrors for upstream TS-Go tests; these are validation artifacts, not production @tsgo-unit source.
The LSP command entrypoint must not exist in the active standalone compiler source tree.
Node executable adapter for the standalone compiler; it delegates to the TS-Go cmd/tsgo main port and has no upstream Go unit.
Package public API barrel for embedding the standalone compiler; it re-exports ported compiler surfaces and has no upstream Go unit.
TypeScript-compatible public service APIs for embedding TSTS; additive host surface with no upstream Go unit.
Additive compiler extension host public API; target-neutral infrastructure with no upstream Go unit.
Generated AST infrastructure emitted by porter:ast from packages/tsts/schema/tsgo/*; carries @tsgo-generated metadata, not @tsgo-unit.
Generated diagnostic message catalog emitted by porter:diagnostics from the pinned diagnostics_generated.go; carries @tsgo-generated metadata, not @tsgo-unit.

Generated Unicode data tables (identifier ID_Start/ID_Continue ranges, Cased/Case_Ignorable ranges, special-casing map) emitted by porter:unicode from the pinned @unicode/unicode-15.1.0 package; carries @tsgo-generated metadata, not @tsgo-unit.
Generated bundled library indexes emitted by bundled:generate from TS-Go internal/bundled/libs; carries @tsgo-generated metadata, not @tsgo-unit.
Generated bundled TypeScript lib declarations copied from TS-Go internal/bundled/libs; carries @tsgo-generated metadata, not @tsgo-unit.
Thin re-export barrels (ast_generated.ts/kind_generated.ts) aliasing the canonical generated/* modules to the scaffolder's Go-file-derived import paths; pure re-exports, no @tsgo-unit.
The Go compatibility layer is authored TypeScript infrastructure, not a TS-Go source unit.
The Go compatibility layer is authored TypeScript infrastructure, not a TS-Go source unit.

Authored host-native facade for the upstream internal/fswatch package (out-of-scope OS file-watch internals: fanotify/FSEvents/kqueue/inotify). Exposes the public surface the execute watcher consumes with a stub that throws TSGO_EXTERNAL_FACADE_UNIMPLEMENTED; not a ported TS-Go unit, so it carries no @tsgo-unit metadata.
Every source file must carry at least one @tsgo-unit unless an explicit policy allows it.

overrides.reason: Fixed-width integer and floating-point behavior must be implemented as an explicit Go-semantics layer.
Operating-system bindings must be implemented through the TypeScript host layer.
File-watch bindings must be implemented through the TypeScript host layer.

policies.reason: Go test units are ported through the test-parity harness, not production compiler scaffolds.
Generated Go files must be regenerated from their upstream source schema or generator.
Upstream repository maintenance tools are not part of the standalone compiler runtime port.
TS-Go command-line test harness files are ported through test-parity infrastructure, not production compiler scaffolds.
TS-Go tsoptions test helpers are ported through test-parity infrastructure, not production compiler scaffolds.
The LSP command entrypoint is outside the standalone compiler porter scope.
JSON-RPC/session API surfaces are language-service/embedder infrastructure, not the standalone compiler runtime port.
Project-service state management belongs to the language-service layer, not the standalone compiler runtime port.
Formatting is an editor/language-service feature and is outside the standalone compiler runtime port.
Upstream test helpers are ported through test-parity infrastructure, not production compiler scaffolds.
Upstream test-runner files are ported through test-parity infrastructure, not production compiler scaffolds.
Fixed-width integer and floating-point behavior must be hand-ported with explicit JavaScript/Tsonic numeric semantics.
OS filesystem bindings must map to the TypeScript/Node host layer, not a literal syscall port.
File watching must map to the TypeScript/Node host layer.

Upstream's vendored file-watch library (fsnotify fork: fanotify/FSEvents/debounce, ~270 units) replaces vfswatch. TSTS does not port the OS-level watch internals; file watching is provided through the TypeScript/Node host layer behind the WatchBackend interface that execute/watcher consumes.
Language-service protocol/server surface is not in the standalone compiler porter scope.
Language-service implementation is not in the standalone compiler porter scope.
Fourslash is a language-service/editor test harness and is not in the standalone compiler porter scope.
